text.title: C# Clean Architecture + CQRS Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Clean Architecture + CQRS pattern for .NET 8/9. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Project references point inward only: API → Application → Domain ← Infrastructure. Domain MUST have zero outward references. Application MUST NOT reference Infrastructure or `Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore`.
rule.rationale: Inward dependency direction keeps Domain stable; any Application-on-Infrastructure reference collapses the layering and re-introduces a big-ball-of-mud.

rule.statement: Business behaviour MUST live on Domain entity methods (e.g. `user.Activate()`). Application handlers orchestrate the call but MUST NOT host the rule (no `if (user.IsActive) throw` in a handler).
rule.rationale: Behaviour in handlers leaves entities as property bags; invariants leak and the layering's value evaporates.

rule.statement: Each use-case MUST live in `Application/Features/<Feature>/<UseCase>/` with a single Command/Query, a single Handler, an optional Validator, and an optional Response — one folder, one use-case.
rule.rationale: One-per-folder makes the agent surface area tiny; mixed folders multiply the cognitive load per change.

rule.statement: Input validation MUST live in a FluentValidation `Validator` on the Command/Query DTO. Domain invariants stay in entity factories. DataAnnotation attributes on DTOs are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Three validation surfaces (annotations + validator + entity) drift; a single FluentValidation site keeps the contract testable.

rule.statement: Domain events that touch other aggregates MUST be dispatched via the Outbox pattern (e.g. MassTransit Outbox) — never inline inside the same `SaveChangesAsync` transaction.
rule.rationale: Inline cross-aggregate handlers roll back the outer transaction on a downstream failure; outbox makes the dispatch atomic with the write and retried independently.

rule.statement: MediatR MUST be reserved for use-cases with cross-cutting needs (validation pipeline, auth pipeline, transaction pipeline, logging). Trivial reads (`GET /health`, simple lookups) MUST NOT be wrapped in Commands/Queries.
rule.rationale: Every dispatch pays handler resolution + pipeline overhead; wrapping `/health` adds latency for no benefit.
